Question: Define a point.
Answer: A point has zero dimension and is represented by a dot.

Question: Define a line.
Answer: A line has one dimension and extends infinitely in both directions.

Question: Define a line segment.
Answer: A part of a line with two endpoints.

Question: Define a ray.
Answer: A part of a line with one endpoint extending infinitely in one direction.

Question: Define a plane.
Answer: A flat surface with two dimensions that extends infinitely.

Question: Define parallel lines.
Answer: Two lines in the same plane that never meet.

Question: Define perpendicular lines.
Answer: Two lines that intersect at a right angle (90°).

Question: Define a polygon.
Answer: A closed figure made of straight line segments.

Question: Name three types of triangles based on sides.
Answer: Equilateral

Question: Name three types of triangles based on angles.
Answer: Acute

Question: Define a circle.
Answer: Set of all points equidistant from a fixed point called the center.

Question: Define radius of a circle.
Answer: Distance from the center to any point on the circle.

Question: Define diameter of a circle.
Answer: A line passing through the center with endpoints on the circle; longest chord.

Question: Define chord of a circle.
Answer: A line segment connecting two points on a circle.

Question: Define tangent of a circle.
Answer: A line touching the circle at exactly one point.

Question: Define secant of a circle.
Answer: A line intersecting the circle at two points.

Question: Define angle.
Answer: An angle is formed by two rays with a common endpoint called vertex.

Question: Define right angle.
Answer: An angle measuring exactly 90°.

Question: Define acute angle.
Answer: An angle measuring less than 90°.

Question: Define obtuse angle.
Answer: An angle measuring more than 90° but less than 180°.

Question: Define straight angle.
Answer: An angle measuring exactly 180°.

Question: Define complementary angles.
Answer: Two angles whose sum is 90°.

Question: Define supplementary angles.
Answer: Two angles whose sum is 180°.

Question: Define adjacent angles.
Answer: Two angles sharing a common vertex and side.

Question: Define vertically opposite angles.
Answer: Angles opposite to each other when two lines intersect; equal in measure.

Question: Define a rectangle.
Answer: A quadrilateral with opposite sides equal and four right angles.

Question: Define a square.
Answer: A quadrilateral with all sides equal and four right angles.

Question: Define a parallelogram.
Answer: A quadrilateral with both pairs of opposite sides parallel.

Question: Define a rhombus.
Answer: A quadrilateral with all sides equal and opposite angles equal.

Question: Define a trapezium.
Answer: A quadrilateral with exactly one pair of opposite sides parallel.

Question: Define a median of a triangle.
Answer: A line joining a vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side.

Question: Define an altitude of a triangle.
Answer: A perpendicular from a vertex to the opposite side or its extension.

Question: Define a sector of a circle.
Answer: Region enclosed by two radii and the corresponding arc.

Question: Define a segment of a circle.
Answer: Region enclosed by a chord and the corresponding arc.

Question: Define a line of symmetry.
Answer: A line that divides a figure into two mirror images.

Question: Define concave polygon.
Answer: A polygon with at least one interior angle greater than 180°.

Question: Define convex polygon.
Answer: A polygon with all interior angles less than 180°.

Question: Define regular polygon.
Answer: A polygon with all sides and all angles equal.

Question: Define irregular polygon.
Answer: A polygon with sides and angles not equal.

Question: Define transversal.
Answer: A line that intersects two or more lines at distinct points.

Question: State the sum of interior angles of a triangle.
State the sum of interior angles of a quadrilateral.
State the sum of interior angles of an n-sided polygon.
State the sum of exterior angles of any polygon.
Define corresponding angles.
Answer: Angles in the same relative position when a transversal intersects parallel lines.

Question: Define alternate interior angles.
Answer: Angles on opposite sides of transversal but inside the parallel lines.

Question: Define perpendicular bisector.
Answer: A line perpendicular to a segment passing through its midpoint.

Question: Define angle bisector.
Answer: A line dividing an angle into two equal angles.

Answer: Examples: square tiles

Question: Explain how to identify an irregular polygon in daily life.
Answer: Examples: kite-shaped roofs
